evals.prompt: I have a prompt that generates technical diagrams but the quality is inconsistent. Some come out with garbled text, wrong colors. I want to systematically improve it. I have a Gemini API key and about 30 example topics. Can you help me set up an automated optimization loop?
evals.expected_output: Reads SKILL.md, loads config.yaml, detects existing criteria, asks about cycles, runs the optimization loop
expectations: Reads SKILL.md and follows the step-by-step procedure defined in it
Loads and validates config.yaml before attempting to run
Detects that evaluation criteria already exist in config.yaml and does NOT regenerate them
Asks the user how many cycles to run (or confirms before starting)
Provides the correct autoresearch.py command with --config config.yaml flag

evals.prompt: I already have a config.yaml with criteria defined for my code review prompt optimizer. Just run 5 cycles for me and show me the dashboard when done.
evals.expected_output: Skips criteria setup, runs exactly 5 cycles, starts the dashboard
expectations: Skips criteria generation/review entirely since user says criteria exist
Uses the --cycles 5 flag to run exactly 5 optimization cycles
Offers to start the dashboard (dashboard.py) after cycles complete
Identifies the correct config file for the code review use case

evals.prompt: I want to create an optimization loop for my email subject line generator. It uses OpenAI's API to generate subject lines and I want to evaluate them on click-worthiness, clarity, and length. Can you set everything up from scratch?
evals.expected_output: Creates a complete new config YAML for email subject lines with OpenAI backend, criteria, topics, and initial prompt
expectations: Creates a new config YAML with openai_text as the generation backend
Defines evaluation criteria matching the user's three requirements: click-worthiness, clarity, and length
Includes realistic email subject line topics as test inputs
Creates an initial prompt.txt as a starting point for optimization
Sets up or documents the data directory structure needed for the optimization loop
